calendar.matters:
For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp_109.5)
Off Calendar Nov-29-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Colin Bedell, Tel. 415.756.7209, petitioner appearing on his own behalf;
Sabrina Berdux Cohen, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3929, for respondents.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling issued for the November 17, 2017 hearing, as modified:

PETITIONER COLIN BEDELL'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is DENIED. There is ample substantial evidence, in the form of the testimony of Grace Shohet, supporting the hearing officer's determinations in the August 30, 2017 statement of decision that Mr. Bedell's dog Sandy is a threat to public safety and Mr. Bedell, despite multiple warnings and admonishments, is unable to prevent Sandy from being a threat to public safety. By prior orders Sandy had previously been declared a vicious and dangerous dog and was required to be muzzled and restrained with a chain whenever she was outside and not in a secure enclosure per SFHC 42.2(d). At the August 24, 2017 hearing Ms. Sohet testified that on August 3, 2017 Sandy bit her dog Scout, resulting in significant injuries to Scout. Mr. Bedell has not shown that there were any irregularities or errors in the August 24 hearing or the August 30 statement of decision that would support the vacating of the August 30 decision. While it is regrettable that the Sandy will be humanely destroyed and Mr. Bedell will lose a pet that is very important to him, there is no basis for this court to disturb the hearing officer's order.

MODIFICATION: A stay of one week is granted to allow petitioner to file an appeal or writ seeking relief from the Court of Appeal.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR#5525, e-mail: vgross@sftc.org.

For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp_109.5)
Off Calendar Nov-17-2017 Continued To Nov-29-17 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Colin E. Bedell, In Propria Persona ((415-756-7309) appears as self-represented petitioner

Deputy City Attorney, Margaret Baumgartner of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3859) appears as counsel for respondent

Court states the tentative ruling for the benefit of the petitioner. The cause is argued by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court sets aside and holds in abeyance the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER COLIN BEDELL'S Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (CCP_109.5) is CONTINUED to November 29, 2017 at 1:30 PM in Department 302/Law and Motion. Court requests that the City verify whether or not there is an alternative to humane destruction, such as adoption.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR No. 12355, e-mail: lgillian@sftc.org. (L&M302)
